exercises.header: ο φίλος → ___ φίλος (accusative article)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns take τον in the accusative. ο → τον.

exercises.header: η μητέρα → ___ μητέρα (accusative article)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns take την in the accusative. η → την.

exercises.header: το σπίτι → ___ σπίτι (accusative article)
exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns do not change in the accusative. το stays το.

exercises.header: ο δάσκαλος → ___ δάσκαλος (accusative article)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns take τον in the accusative. ο → τον.

exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns take την in the accusative. η → την.

exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns do not change in the accusative. το stays το.

exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns take τον in the accusative. ο → τον.

exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns take τον in the accusative. ο → τον.

exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns take την in the accusative. η → την.

exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns do not change in the accusative. το stays το.

exercises.header: ο αδελφός → ___ αδελφός (accusative article)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns take τον in the accusative. ο → τον.

exercises.header: η αδελφή → ___ αδελφή (accusative article)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns take την in the accusative. η → την.

exercises.header: ο φίλος → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ος drop the -ς in the accusative: φίλος → φίλο.

exercises.header: ο δάσκαλος → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ος drop the -ς in the accusative: δάσκαλος → δάσκαλο.

exercises.header: ο αδελφός → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ος drop the -ς in the accusative: αδελφός → αδελφό.

exercises.header: ο γιατρός → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ος drop the -ς in the accusative: γιατρός → γιατρό.

exercises.header: ο άνθρωπος → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ος drop the -ς in the accusative: άνθρωπος → άνθρωπο.

exercises.header: ο πατέρας → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ας drop the -ς in the accusative: πατέρας → πατέρα.

exercises.header: ο άντρας → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ας drop the -ς in the accusative: άντρας → άντρα.

exercises.header: ο νέος → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ος drop the -ς in the accusative: νέος → νέο.

exercises.header: ο μαθητής → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ης drop the -ς in the accusative: μαθητής → μαθητή.

exercises.header: ο πελάτης → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ης drop the -ς in the accusative: πελάτης → πελάτη.

exercises.header: ο εργάτης → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ης drop the -ς in the accusative: εργάτης → εργάτη.

exercises.header: ο πολίτης → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ης drop the -ς in the accusative: πολίτης → πολίτη.

exercises.header: ο τουρίστας → τον ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Masculine nouns ending in -ας drop the -ς in the accusative: τουρίστας → τουρίστα.

exercises.header: η μητέρα → την ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -α keep the same ending in the accusative: μητέρα → μητέρα.

exercises.header: η τράπεζα → την ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -α keep the same ending in the accusative: τράπεζα → τράπεζα.

exercises.header: η γυναίκα → την ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -α keep the same ending in the accusative: γυναίκα → γυναίκα.

exercises.header: η πόλη → την ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -η keep the same ending in the accusative: πόλη → πόλη.

exercises.header: η ζωή → την ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -η keep the same ending in the accusative: ζωή → ζωή.

exercises.header: η αδελφή → την ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -η keep the same ending in the accusative: αδελφή → αδελφή.

exercises.header: η δουλειά → την ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -ά keep the same ending in the accusative: δουλειά → δουλειά.

exercises.header: η εφημερίδα → την ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -α keep the same ending in the accusative: εφημερίδα → εφημερίδα.

exercises.header: η φωτογραφία → την ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -α/-ία keep the same ending in the accusative: φωτογραφία → φωτογραφία.

exercises.header: το σπίτι → το ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns do not change at all in the accusative: σπίτι → σπίτι.

exercises.header: το βιβλίο → το ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns do not change at all in the accusative: βιβλίο → βιβλίο.

exercises.header: το παιδί → το ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns do not change at all in the accusative: παιδί → παιδί.

exercises.header: το αυτοκίνητο → το ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns do not change at all in the accusative: αυτοκίνητο → αυτοκίνητο.

exercises.header: το πρόβλημα → το ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns do not change at all in the accusative: πρόβλημα → πρόβλημα.

exercises.header: το όνομα → το ___ (noun ending in accusative)
exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns do not change at all in the accusative: όνομα → όνομα.

exercises.header: ο φίλος → accusative: ___
exercises.explanation: ο φίλος is masculine: article ο→τον, noun drops -ς: φίλος→φίλο. Full accusative: τον φίλο.

exercises.header: ο δάσκαλος → accusative: ___
exercises.explanation: ο δάσκαλος is masculine: article ο→τον, noun drops -ς: δάσκαλος→δάσκαλο. Full accusative: τον δάσκαλο.

exercises.header: ο πατέρας → accusative: ___
exercises.explanation: ο πατέρας is masculine: article ο→τον, noun drops -ς: πατέρας→πατέρα. Full accusative: τον πατέρα.

exercises.header: ο μαθητής → accusative: ___
exercises.explanation: ο μαθητής is masculine: article ο→τον, noun drops -ς: μαθητής→μαθητή. Full accusative: τον μαθητή.

exercises.header: η μητέρα → accusative: ___
exercises.explanation: η μητέρα is feminine: article η→την, noun keeps -α: μητέρα→μητέρα. Full accusative: την μητέρα.

exercises.header: η πόλη → accusative: ___
exercises.explanation: η πόλη is feminine: article η→την, noun keeps -η: πόλη→πόλη. Full accusative: την πόλη.

exercises.header: η γυναίκα → accusative: ___
exercises.explanation: η γυναίκα is feminine: article η→την, noun keeps -α: γυναίκα→γυναίκα. Full accusative: την γυναίκα.

exercises.header: το σπίτι → accusative: ___
exercises.explanation: το σπίτι is neuter: nothing changes at all. Full accusative: το σπίτι.

exercises.header: το βιβλίο → accusative: ___
exercises.explanation: το βιβλίο is neuter: nothing changes at all. Full accusative: το βιβλίο.

exercises.header: το παιδί → accusative: ___
exercises.explanation: το παιδί is neuter: nothing changes at all. Full accusative: το παιδί.
